raw_consumer_statement: I made a payment worth {$43.00} and another of {$26.00} on the XXXX and XXXX. It was the weekend so American express wouldnt charge it. On the XXXX, I was charged a ACH NSF fee worth {$29.00} for the payment for {$43.00} and another as well worth {$29.00} for the {$26.00} payment. Every-time I have spoken with a customer service representative, they have said that Amex only retries the payments every 2-3 business days. XXXX, single, time, as well as the supervisor whom i begged to connect to. Then on the XXXX, they again charged me {$29.00} and another {$29.00} for my {$43.00} payment and the {$26.00} payment. On my Amex statement, it confirms the XXXX charges of {$29.00} on the XXXX, but not the XXXX. Everyone at Amex said they did not see that charge, and they never said it was from them. So i called my bank for the XXXX time, and they said that Amex XXXX attempted to charge the payment on the XXXX, and the XXXX. So {$58.00} disappeared into thin air, i should also mention that the first charge on the XXXX that is verified by my card statement, and the one on the XXXX both say the same thing, ACH NSF Fee In the amount AMEX EPAYMENT So that makes me think that all 4 of those charges are from Amex, or my bank. Yet my bank is saying it is because Amex tried to charge me, but the charge isnt showing up to anyone in charge of customer support at Amex. There is no way to provide images and they just pretend like they arent responsible for the charge, and my bank as well. I bank with XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. I have all the transaction IDs and screenshots of customer support.
